methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
OpenStar is a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Ratu Mataira and Thomas Berry that has raised $35M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe comprising one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private companies in the same sector, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method, anchoring to the $35M total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value ($10.0B base), applies a typical 2.5x multiplier for progression from seed to next stage, yielding $25.0B. The Peer-Median method examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A). The median valuation across these three peers is $150M, with a range of $110M–$200M.

The triangulation produces a wide implied range: $128M at the low end (peer-median floor), $12.6B at the median, and $28.7B at the high end (stage-progression ceiling). The disparity reflects material uncertainty in stage-specific benchmarking for seed-stage companies in frontier energy and the appropriateness of stage-progression multiples given limited precedent in this subsector. The peer-median method is more conservative and defensible given direct comparability; the stage-progression method is highly sensitive to baseline assumptions and stage-step assumptions. Further diligence on unit economics and market timing would be necessary to narrow this range.
